output:
I‚Äôm checking the current branch state and the story-machine files first so I can verify what‚Äôs already changed, what still fails, and whether story `25-2-story-machine` is actually complete or needs follow-up fixes.I have the current implementation in view. Next I‚Äôm validating it against the actual story artifact and running the relevant checks so I can tell whether there‚Äôs still implementation work or just verification and cleanup left.The story-machine test file is green and the implementation matches the story spec. I‚Äôm finishing the remaining acceptance checks now: build completion, lint, boundary/size checks, and the full Vitest run to see whether AC2 is truly satisfied on the current tree.The local acceptance checks are clean so far: build passed, lint passed, file size is 219 lines, and the boundary import check is clean. The only remaining material gate is the full test suite.The full suite is not green on the current tree. The first failing file is `workflow-run-machine.test.ts`, which means AC2 is not currently true even though the story-machine-specific checks are. I‚Äôm extracting the exact failure and verifying whether it‚Äôs caused by story `25-2` or by downstream work already present in the branch.I isolated the full-suite failures. They‚Äôre both downstream expectations outside `25-2` itself: `workflow-run-machine.test.ts` now expects `allDone` where the current machine correctly returns `halted`, and `workflow-runner.test.ts` still assumes continuation on UNKNOWN per-story errors. I‚Äôm checking whether this story also expects a proof artifact/status update before I close it out.`25-2` is functionally implemented, but its AC2 still fails on this branch because two downstream tests encode the old ‚Äúcontinue on non-halt story errors‚Äù behavior. I‚Äôm reading those failing expectations now; if they are just stale assertions, I‚Äôll update them so the suite matches the actual story-level semantics and closes the acceptance gap.I‚Äôve confirmed both failures are stale expectations, not implementation bugs. I‚Äôm updating those tests to match the accepted story-level rule: a non-halt per-story task error is still a story error, so it halts that story and propagates up to halted at run level.I‚Äôm not changing machine behavior here. The code already enforces the story-level rule from AC15; the remaining failures are stale tests that still expect the pre-25-2 continuation semantics. I‚Äôm updating those expectations now so the suite matches the implemented contract.The downstream expectations are patched. I‚Äôm rerunning the two affected test files first, then the full Vitest suite to confirm AC2 is actually true on the current branch rather than just in the story document.The targeted fixes are green. I‚Äôm waiting on the full suite now; once that finishes I‚Äôll do a quick final status check and then close out with exactly what changed and what remains inconsistent in the repo metadata.The implementation and acceptance gates are now green. I‚Äôm doing one last repo-state check before I decide whether it‚Äôs safe to publish a focused commit for just the story-machine work, because the workspace also contains unrelated in-progress changes.I can publish just the focused story-machine work without touching the unrelated local changes, but it requires a careful partial-commit flow because the tree is already dirty and `master` is ahead of `origin/master`. I‚Äôm staging only the story-related files, stashing everything else, then I‚Äôll commit, rebase, push, and restore the unrelated worktree changes.The unrelated worktree changes are stashed away safely. I‚Äôm committing only the story-machine implementation plus the downstream expectation fixes needed to make AC2 true.The partial commit is staged, but `git commit` is blocked by a stale `.git/index.lock`. I‚Äôm checking whether there‚Äôs an active git process; if not, I‚Äôll remove the stale lock and continue.There‚Äôs no active git process and the lock file is already gone, so this was transient. I‚Äôm retrying the commit, then I‚Äôll rebase and push.The focused commit is in place. Next step is syncing `master` with `origin/master`, pushing the new commit set, and then restoring the unrelated local changes from the stash.The branch is rebased and currently `ahead 2`; the remaining tracked noise is only `.claude/worktrees/agent-a933b425`, which is separate from the story commit. I‚Äôm waiting for the push to finish, then I‚Äôll restore the stashed local work and confirm branch sync status.The push completed successfully. I‚Äôm restoring the pre-existing local changes from the stash now, then I‚Äôll verify that `master` is back to up to date with `origin/master` and that your unrelated worktree state is preserved.Implemented `25-2-story-machine` in [workflow-story-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts) and aligned the tests with the accepted story-level error semantics. The story machine now runs `storyFlow` sequentially, delegates gate steps to `gateMachine`, routes `INTERRUPT` to `interrupted`, emits `story-done` on clean completion, and halts on any plain-task error, including non-halt dispatch errors. I also updated [workflow-story-machine.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-story-machine.test.ts), [workflow-run-machine.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-run-machine.test.ts), and [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts) so the suite matches that contract.

Verification is green: `npm run build` passed, `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts src/lib/__tests__/workflow-story-machine.test.ts` passed, `wc -l` reports 219 lines for the story machine, the forbidden-import check is clean, the story-machine test file passes `12/12`, and the full suite passes `5163/5163`. I committed and pushed the work as `efdfd4d` (`Implement story machine semantics`). `git status -sb` now shows `master...origin/master` with the pre-existing unrelated local changes restored.

acceptanceCriteria.description: [x] **Given** the codebase after implementation, **When** `npm run build` is executed in the project root, **Then** it exits with code 0 and produces no TypeScript errors in the terminal output. <!-- verification: `npm run build` exits 0 -->

[x] **Given** the full test suite, **When** `npx vitest run` is executed in the project root, **Then** the summary line shows zero failures and all tests pass ‚Äî no regressions from adding the story machine. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine with a single plain task step configured to succeed, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the machine reaches the `done` final state and the task was dispatched exactly once. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*machine.*done" or "story.*single.*task" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine with three plain task steps in sequence, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming all three tasks were dispatched in order and the machine reaches `done` with `tasksCompleted` equal to 3. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*sequential" or "story.*three.*steps" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine with a gate step where all check tasks pass, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the gate was invoked, the gate passed, and the machine reaches `done` ‚Äî the story machine correctly delegates to the gate machine. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*gate.*pass" or "story.*gate.*done" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine with a gate step that halts (e.g., max retries exceeded), **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the machine reaches the `halted` final state and the gate's output (errors, halted flag) is merged into the story context. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*gate.*halt" or "story.*gate.*maxed" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine where a plain task dispatch throws a halt error (e.g., `RATE_LIMIT`), **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the machine transitions to `halted` and the error is recorded in context. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*halt.*error" or "story.*RATE_LIMIT" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine and then sends an `INTERRUPT` event, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the machine reaches the `interrupted` final state. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*interrupted" or "story.*INTERRUPT" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates and starts a story machine with a null task step (agent: null), **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the null task was dispatched through the null task actor path, not the agent dispatch actor. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*null.*task" or "story.*nullTask" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates a story machine and runs it to completion with all steps succeeding, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the machine output includes updated `workflowState`, accumulated `errors`, `tasksCompleted` count, `lastContract`, `accumulatedCostUsd`, and `halted` flag ‚Äî matching the `StoryFlowOutput` type. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*output" or "story.*result" -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates a story machine with a mix of plain task, gate, and plain task steps in sequence, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the machine processes task ‚Üí gate ‚Üí task in order, the gate output is merged into the story context, and subsequent tasks receive the updated context. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*mixed.*steps" or "story.*task.*gate.*task" -->

[x] **Given** the story machine definition file, **When** `npx eslint` is run against it, **Then** it exits with code 0 and produces no lint errors. <!-- verification: `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts` exits 0 -->

[x] **Given** the story machine definition file, **When** its line count is checked, **Then** it contains <= 300 lines (per NFR18 file size constraint). <!-- verification: `wc -l src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts` shows <= 300 -->

[x] **Given** the story machine source file, **When** its imports are inspected, **Then** it does not import from `workflow-runner.ts`, `workflow-visualizer.ts`, or `workflow-persistence.ts` ‚Äî boundary rules are respected. <!-- verification: `grep "from.*workflow-runner\|from.*workflow-visualizer\|from.*workflow-persistence" src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts` returns no matches -->

[x] **Given** a test that creates a story machine where a plain task throws a non-halt error, **When** `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` is executed, **Then** verbose output shows a passing test confirming the error is recorded in the context `errors` array and the machine transitions to `halted` (story-level errors halt the story, unlike gate-level where non-halt errors allow continuation). <!-- verification: `npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` shows a passing test matching "story.*non-halt.*error" or "story.*error.*recorded" -->
